## Overview

This document is a **descriptive catalog** of the canonical parser parquets
for **{{< meta source-title >}}**. Shows what's in the data — schema,
coverage, distributions, preservation. **Does not interpret** content (no
sentiment, clustering, topic detection). For qualitative analysis, see
project `~/AI Interaction Analysis/`.

```{python}
#| label: overview-totals
#| code-summary: "Totals per table"

_rows = [
    {"Table": f"{SOURCE_KEY}_conversations.parquet", "Rows": fmt_int(table_count(con, "conversations"))},
    {"Table": f"{SOURCE_KEY}_messages.parquet",      "Rows": fmt_int(table_count(con, "messages"))},
    {"Table": f"{SOURCE_KEY}_tool_events.parquet",   "Rows": fmt_int(table_count(con, "tool_events"))},
    {"Table": f"{SOURCE_KEY}_branches.parquet",      "Rows": fmt_int(table_count(con, "branches"))},
]
for _aux in AUX_TABLES:
    _rows.append({"Table": f"{SOURCE_KEY}_{_aux}.parquet", "Rows": fmt_int(table_count(con, _aux))})
show_df(pd.DataFrame(_rows))
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-period

_period = con.execute("""
    SELECT MIN(created_at) AS oldest, MAX(created_at) AS newest, MAX(updated_at) AS last_update
    FROM conversations
""").df()
_oldest = _period["oldest"].iloc[0]
_newest = _period["newest"].iloc[0]
_last = _period["last_update"].iloc[0]

_meta_rows = [
    {"Field": "Creation period", "Value": f"{_oldest:%Y-%m-%d} → {_newest:%Y-%m-%d}" if pd.notna(_oldest) else "—"},
    {"Field": "Last update", "Value": f"{_last:%Y-%m-%d %H:%M}" if pd.notna(_last) else "—"},
]
if PROCESSED.exists():
    import datetime as _dt
    _mt = max((p.stat().st_mtime for p in PROCESSED.glob(f"{SOURCE_KEY}_*.parquet")), default=None)
    if _mt:
        _meta_rows.append({"Field": "Parquets generated at", "Value": _dt.datetime.fromtimestamp(_mt).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")})
    _size = sum(p.stat().st_size for p in PROCESSED.glob(f"{SOURCE_KEY}_*.parquet"))
    _meta_rows.append({"Field": "Size on disk", "Value": f"{_size/1024/1024:.1f} MB"})
show_df(pd.DataFrame(_meta_rows))
```

# 1. Available data

Each canonical table has its own schema. Here we show the fields, their
coverage in this base (how many rows have the field populated) and
diversified samples.

## 1.1 Conversations

The conversations table is the central unit. Each row represents a chat,
session or notebook — with metadata (title, model, project, dates) and
preservation fields created by the parser (`is_preserved_missing`,
`last_seen_in_server`).

```{python}
#| label: conv-schema
#| code-summary: "Coverage per field — conversations"

_t = table_count(con, "conversations")
_cov = con.execute(f"""
    SELECT
        COUNT(title) AS title,
        COUNT(model) AS model,
        COUNT("mode") AS mode,
        COUNT(project) AS project,
        COUNT(project_id) AS project_id,
        COUNT(gizmo_id) AS gizmo_id,
        SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) AS preserved,
        COUNT(last_seen_in_server) AS last_seen,
        SUM(is_pinned::INT) AS pinned,
        SUM(is_archived::INT) AS archived,
        SUM(is_temporary::INT) AS temporary
    FROM conversations
""").df()

def _conv_cov(field):
    if field in _cov.columns:
        n = _cov[field].iloc[0]
    else:
        # Conditional column (summary, settings_json) — query directly
        n = con.execute(f'SELECT COUNT("{field}") FROM conversations').fetchone()[0]
    return fmt_pct(int(n) if pd.notna(n) else 0, _t)

_schema_rows = [
    {"Field": "conversation_id", "Type": "str",       "Description": "Unique ID (platform UUID/hash)",                          "Coverage": fmt_pct(_t, _t)},
    {"Field": "source",          "Type": "str",       "Description": f"Always '{SOURCE_KEY}' in this table",                    "Coverage": fmt_pct(_t, _t)},
    {"Field": "title",           "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Title generated by server or user",                       "Coverage": _conv_cov("title")},
    {"Field": "created_at",      "Type": "timestamp", "Description": "When the conv was created",                               "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "updated_at",      "Type": "timestamp", "Description": "Last msg or rename (server bumps on some platforms)",     "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "message_count",   "Type": "int",       "Description": "Visible msgs (user+assistant non-hidden)",                "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "model",           "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Last assistant model_slug in the conv",                   "Coverage": _conv_cov("model")},
    {"Field": "mode",            "Type": "str?",      "Description": "chat / search / research / copilot / concise / dalle / cli","Coverage": _conv_cov("mode")},
    {"Field": "project",         "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Project/folder name in the sidebar",                      "Coverage": _conv_cov("project")},
    {"Field": "project_id",      "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Project ID",                                              "Coverage": _conv_cov("project_id")},
    {"Field": "gizmo_id",        "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Linked custom GPT/agent",                                 "Coverage": _conv_cov("gizmo_id")},
    {"Field": "is_preserved_missing","Type":"bool",   "Description": "True if disappeared from server (preserved locally)",     "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_cov['preserved'].iloc[0])} flagged"},
    {"Field": "last_seen_in_server","Type":"timestamp","Description": "Date of last run when server returned it",               "Coverage": _conv_cov("last_seen")},
    {"Field": "is_pinned",       "Type": "bool",      "Description": "Pin (depends on platform — some don't expose)",           "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_cov['pinned'].iloc[0])} flagged"},
    {"Field": "is_archived",     "Type": "bool",      "Description": "Archive (depends on platform)",                           "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_cov['archived'].iloc[0])} flagged"},
    {"Field": "is_temporary",    "Type": "bool",      "Description": "Temporary chat (ephemeral — Claude.ai exposes)",          "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_cov['temporary'].iloc[0])} flagged"},
]
if has_col(con, "conversations", "interaction_type"):
    _it_human = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations WHERE interaction_type='human_ai'").fetchone()[0]
    _it_ai = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations WHERE interaction_type='ai_ai'").fetchone()[0]
    _schema_rows.append({"Field": "interaction_type", "Type": "str", "Description": "human_ai (default) or ai_ai (subagent CLI)", "Coverage": f"human_ai={_it_human}, ai_ai={_it_ai}"})
if has_col(con, "conversations", "account"):
    _n_acc = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT account) FROM conversations WHERE account IS NOT NULL").fetchone()[0]
    _schema_rows.append({"Field": "account", "Type": "str?", "Description": "Account on the platform (multi-account)", "Coverage": f"{_n_acc} distinct"})
if has_col(con, "conversations", "summary"):
    _schema_rows.append({"Field": "summary", "Type": "str?", "Description": "Auto-summary generated by the platform", "Coverage": _conv_cov("summary") if has_col(con, "conversations", "summary") else "—"})
if has_col(con, "conversations", "settings_json"):
    _schema_rows.append({"Field": "settings_json", "Type": "str?", "Description": "Feature flags / conv settings (JSON)", "Coverage": _conv_cov("settings_json") if has_col(con, "conversations", "settings_json") else "—"})
if has_col(con, "conversations", "capture_method"):
    _schema_rows.append({"Field": "capture_method", "Type": "str", "Description": "extractor / manual_clipping_obsidian / manual_copypaste / manual_terminal_cc", "Coverage": fmt_pct(_t, _t)})

show_df(pd.DataFrame(_schema_rows))
```

```{python}
#| label: conv-sample
#| code-summary: "Diversified sample — conversations"
#| column: page

_ctes = []
_add = lambda name, q: _ctes.append((name, q))
_add("recent",   "SELECT * FROM conversations ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1")
_add("longest",  "SELECT * FROM conversations ORDER BY message_count DESC LIMIT 1")
_add("shortest", "SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE message_count > 0 ORDER BY message_count ASC LIMIT 1")
if safe_int(_cov["preserved"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _add("preserved", "SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE is_preserved_missing=TRUE ORDER BY last_seen_in_server LIMIT 1")
if safe_int(_cov["pinned"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _add("pinned", "SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE is_pinned=TRUE LIMIT 1")
if safe_int(_cov["project"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _add("with_project", "SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE project IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1")
if safe_int(_cov["model"].iloc[0]) < _t:
    _add("no_model", "SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE model IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1")

_union = " UNION ALL BY NAME ".join(f"({q})" for _, q in _ctes)
_sample_cols = "substr(conversation_id, 1, 8) AS conv_id, title, message_count AS msgs, model, project, is_preserved_missing AS preserved, is_pinned AS pinned, strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created"
_sample = con.execute(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (conversation_id) {_sample_cols}
    FROM ({_union})
    ORDER BY conversation_id
""").df()
show_df(_sample)
```

## 1.2 Messages

User/assistant messages. Messages with `role=tool` become `tool_events`
(don't appear here). Internal messages (assistant calling tool) are
preserved with `is_hidden=True` on some platforms.

```{python}
#| label: msg-schema
#| code-summary: "Coverage per field — messages"

_mt = table_count(con, "messages")
_mcov = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        COUNT(model) AS model,
        COUNT(thinking) AS thinking,
        COUNT(token_count) AS token_count,
        COUNT(word_count) AS word_count,
        COUNT(content_types) AS content_types,
        COUNT(asset_paths) AS asset_paths,
        COUNT(attachment_names) AS attachment_names,
        COUNT(finish_reason) AS finish_reason,
        SUM(is_hidden::INT) AS hidden,
        SUM(is_voice::INT) AS voice
    FROM messages
""").df()

def _msg_cov(f):
    n = _mcov[f].iloc[0]
    return fmt_pct(int(n) if pd.notna(n) else 0, _mt)

_msg_rows = [
    {"Field": "message_id",       "Type": "str",       "Description": "Unique ID", "Coverage": fmt_pct(_mt, _mt)},
    {"Field": "conversation_id",  "Type": "str",       "Description": "FK to conversations", "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "branch_id",        "Type": "str",       "Description": "FK to branches (`<conv>_main` by default)", "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "sequence",         "Type": "int",       "Description": "Chronological order (1,2,3...)", "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "role",             "Type": "str",       "Description": "user / assistant / system", "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "content",          "Type": "str",       "Description": "Consolidated text", "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "model",            "Type": "str?",      "Description": "model_slug (NULL for role=user)", "Coverage": _msg_cov("model")},
    {"Field": "created_at",       "Type": "timestamp", "Description": "Msg timestamp", "Coverage": "100%"},
    {"Field": "thinking",         "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Reasoning blocks (Claude reasoning, DeepSeek R1, Gemini thoughts)", "Coverage": _msg_cov("thinking")},
    {"Field": "token_count",      "Type": "int?",      "Description": "Token usage (DeepSeek populates ~98%, others sparse)", "Coverage": _msg_cov("token_count")},
    {"Field": "word_count",       "Type": "int",       "Description": "Words in content", "Coverage": _msg_cov("word_count")},
    {"Field": "content_types",    "Type": "str?",      "Description": "CSV of markers (text/code/dalle/image_upload/...)", "Coverage": _msg_cov("content_types")},
    {"Field": "asset_paths",      "Type": "list?",     "Description": "Resolved paths of assets (uploads, generated)", "Coverage": _msg_cov("asset_paths")},
    {"Field": "attachment_names", "Type": "str?",      "Description": "Names of attached files", "Coverage": _msg_cov("attachment_names")},
    {"Field": "finish_reason",    "Type": "str?",      "Description": "stop / max_tokens / unknown / interrupted", "Coverage": _msg_cov("finish_reason")},
    {"Field": "is_hidden",        "Type": "bool",      "Description": "True if internal (preserved non user-facing)", "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_mcov['hidden'].iloc[0]):,} flagged"},
    {"Field": "is_voice",         "Type": "bool",      "Description": "True if voice origin (audio_transcription)", "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_mcov['voice'].iloc[0]):,} flagged"},
]
if has_col(con, "messages", "citations_json"):
    _n_cit = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(citations_json) FROM messages").fetchone()[0]
    _msg_rows.append({"Field": "citations_json", "Type": "str?", "Description": "Citations in text blocks (JSON)", "Coverage": fmt_pct(int(_n_cit), _mt)})
if has_col(con, "messages", "attachments_json"):
    _n_att = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(attachments_json) FROM messages").fetchone()[0]
    _msg_rows.append({"Field": "attachments_json", "Type": "str?", "Description": "Attachments with extracted_content inline", "Coverage": fmt_pct(int(_n_att), _mt)})
if has_col(con, "messages", "start_timestamp"):
    _n_lat = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(start_timestamp) FROM messages WHERE stop_timestamp IS NOT NULL").fetchone()[0]
    _msg_rows.append({"Field": "start/stop_timestamp", "Type": "timestamp?", "Description": "Latency per block (Claude.ai covers ~98%)", "Coverage": fmt_pct(int(_n_lat), _mt)})

show_df(pd.DataFrame(_msg_rows))
```

```{python}
#| label: msg-sample
#| code-summary: "Diversified sample — messages"
#| column: page

_msg_ctes = []
_msg_ctes.append(("recent_user",      "SELECT * FROM messages WHERE role='user' AND NOT is_hidden ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"))
_msg_ctes.append(("recent_assistant", "SELECT * FROM messages WHERE role='assistant' AND NOT is_hidden ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"))
if safe_int(_mcov["thinking"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _msg_ctes.append(("with_thinking", "SELECT * FROM messages WHERE thinking IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"))
if safe_int(_mcov["voice"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _msg_ctes.append(("voice", "SELECT * FROM messages WHERE is_voice=TRUE LIMIT 1"))
if safe_int(_mcov["asset_paths"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _msg_ctes.append(("with_asset", "SELECT * FROM messages WHERE asset_paths IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"))
if safe_int(_mcov["hidden"].iloc[0]) > 0:
    _msg_ctes.append(("hidden", "SELECT * FROM messages WHERE is_hidden=TRUE ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"))

_union = " UNION ALL BY NAME ".join(f"({q})" for _, q in _msg_ctes)
_msg_sample = con.execute(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (message_id)
        substr(message_id, 1, 8) AS msg_id, role,
        substr(content, 1, 60) AS content_preview,
        word_count AS wc, model,
        thinking IS NOT NULL AS has_thinking,
        is_hidden AS hidden, is_voice AS voice,
        asset_paths IS NOT NULL AS has_asset
    FROM ({_union})
""").df()
show_df(_msg_sample)
```

## 1.3 Tool Events

Non-conversational operations: search, code, canvas/artifact, image_generation,
file_search, computer_use, mcp_*, search_result (Perplexity), and CLI-specific
tools (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, etc).

```{python}
#| label: evt-schema
#| code-summary: "Coverage per field — tool_events"

_et = table_count(con, "tool_events")
if _et > 0:
    _ecov = con.execute("""
        SELECT
            COUNT(tool_name) AS tool_name,
            COUNT(file_path) AS file_path,
            COUNT(result) AS result,
            COUNT("command") AS command,
            COUNT(duration_ms) AS duration_ms,
            COUNT(success) AS success,
            COUNT(metadata_json) AS metadata_json
        FROM tool_events
    """).df()
    def _e(f):
        n = _ecov[f].iloc[0]
        return fmt_pct(int(n) if pd.notna(n) else 0, _et)

    _evt_rows = [
        {"Field": "event_id",         "Type": "str",   "Description": "Unique ID", "Coverage": fmt_pct(_et, _et)},
        {"Field": "conversation_id",  "Type": "str",   "Description": "FK", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "message_id",       "Type": "str",   "Description": "Parent msg", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "event_type",       "Type": "str",   "Description": "Category (search/code/artifact/image_generation/...)", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "tool_name",        "Type": "str?",  "Description": "Exact name preserved (browser.search, python, Bash, etc)", "Coverage": _e("tool_name")},
        {"Field": "file_path",        "Type": "str?",  "Description": "Path on disk (DALL-E, computer_use, Edit/Write CLI)", "Coverage": _e("file_path")},
        {"Field": "result",           "Type": "str?",  "Description": "Tool output (snippets, stdout, brief)", "Coverage": _e("result")},
        {"Field": "command",          "Type": "str?",  "Description": "Executed command (Bash CLI tool_events)", "Coverage": _e("command")},
        {"Field": "duration_ms",      "Type": "int?",  "Description": "Duration in ms (Codex correlates function_call ↔ exec_command_end)", "Coverage": _e("duration_ms")},
        {"Field": "success",          "Type": "bool?", "Description": "True/False/None (CLIs)", "Coverage": _e("success")},
        {"Field": "metadata_json",    "Type": "str?",  "Description": "Raw metadata JSON", "Coverage": _e("metadata_json")},
    ]
    show_df(pd.DataFrame(_evt_rows))

    _evt_sample = con.execute("""
        SELECT DISTINCT ON (event_type)
            event_type, tool_name,
            substr(result, 1, 60) AS result_preview,
            file_path IS NOT NULL AS has_file_path,
            duration_ms, success
        FROM tool_events
        ORDER BY event_type
    """).df()
    show_df(_evt_sample)
else:
    show_md("> No tool_events on this platform.")
```

## 1.4 Branches

Linear path in the conv DAG. Conv without fork has 1 branch (`<conv>_main`).
Conv with fork has N branches: `_main` goes from root to the fork, each fork
starts a sub-branch (with `parent_branch_id`).

```{python}
#| label: br-schema
#| code-summary: "Coverage per field — branches"

_bt = table_count(con, "branches")
if _bt > 0:
    _bcov = con.execute("""
        SELECT
            SUM(is_active::INT) AS active,
            SUM((parent_branch_id IS NULL)::INT) AS main,
            SUM((parent_branch_id IS NOT NULL)::INT) AS forks
        FROM branches
    """).df()

    _br_rows = [
        {"Field": "branch_id",        "Type": "str",       "Description": "`<conv>_main` or `<conv>_<root_node>`", "Coverage": fmt_pct(_bt, _bt)},
        {"Field": "conversation_id",  "Type": "str",       "Description": "FK", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "root_message_id",  "Type": "str",       "Description": "First msg of the branch", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "leaf_message_id",  "Type": "str",       "Description": "Last msg following children", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "is_active",        "Type": "bool",      "Description": "Contains `current_node` (1 per conv)", "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_bcov['active'].iloc[0]):,} active"},
        {"Field": "created_at",       "Type": "timestamp", "Description": "create_time of the root", "Coverage": "100%"},
        {"Field": "parent_branch_id", "Type": "str?",      "Description": "NULL for main; points to originating branch for forks", "Coverage": f"{safe_int(_bcov['forks'].iloc[0])} forks / {safe_int(_bcov['main'].iloc[0])} main"},
    ]
    show_df(pd.DataFrame(_br_rows))
else:
    show_md("> No branches on this platform.")
```

# 2. Coverage and gaps

## 2.1 Capture method (extractor vs manual)

The `capture_method` column (schema v3.2) distinguishes data captured by
the automated extractor versus data imported manually (Obsidian Web
Clipper, copy-paste, terminal screenshot).

```{python}
#| label: capture-method
#| code-summary: "capture_method distribution"

if has_col(con, "conversations", "capture_method"):
    _cm = con.execute("""
        SELECT COALESCE(capture_method, 'extractor') AS method, COUNT(*) AS n
        FROM conversations
        GROUP BY method
        ORDER BY n DESC
    """).df()
    show_df(_cm)
    if len(_cm) > 1:
        fig = plotly_bar(
            _cm["method"].tolist(), _cm["n"].tolist(),
            title="Conversations by capture_method",
            color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=300, orientation="h",
        )
        fig.update_layout(yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
        fig.show()
else:
    show_md("> On this platform, all convs are from the extractor (no manual saves imported).")
```

## 2.2 Model coverage

`Message.model` is only populated on `role=assistant` msgs (user msgs are
always NULL — user doesn't pick model). On assistant msgs, depends on the
server including `model_slug` in the response.

```{python}
#| label: model-by-role

_mr = con.execute("""
    SELECT role,
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(model) AS with_model,
        COUNT(*) - COUNT(model) AS without_model,
        ROUND(COUNT(model) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0), 1) AS pct
    FROM messages GROUP BY role ORDER BY role
""").df()
show_df(_mr)
```

## 2.3 Thinking / reasoning coverage

`Message.thinking` captures internal reasoning blocks (Claude reasoning,
DeepSeek R1, Gemini `thoughts`, Qwen QwQ). Platforms that don't expose
reasoning stay zeroed.

```{python}
#| label: thinking-coverage

_th = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE role='assistant') AS total_asst,
        COUNT(thinking) FILTER (WHERE role='assistant') AS with_thinking
    FROM messages
""").df()
_total_asst = int(_th["total_asst"].iloc[0])
_with_th = int(_th["with_thinking"].iloc[0])
show_df(pd.DataFrame([
    {"Metric": "Assistant msgs", "Value": fmt_int(_total_asst)},
    {"Metric": "With thinking",  "Value": fmt_pct(_with_th, _total_asst)},
]))
```

## 2.4 Token count coverage

`Message.token_count` when the server exposes it (DeepSeek 98%, Gemini partial,
others rare).

```{python}
#| label: token-coverage

_tk = con.execute("""
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, COUNT(token_count) AS with_tokens,
        SUM(token_count) AS total_tokens
    FROM messages
""").df()
_total = int(_tk["total"].iloc[0])
_wt = int(_tk["with_tokens"].iloc[0])
_tot_tokens = _tk["total_tokens"].iloc[0]
show_df(pd.DataFrame([
    {"Metric": "Total msgs", "Value": fmt_int(_total)},
    {"Metric": "With token_count", "Value": fmt_pct(_wt, _total)},
    {"Metric": "Total tokens", "Value": fmt_int(_tot_tokens) if pd.notna(_tot_tokens) else "—"},
]))
```

## 2.5 Latency per msg (start/stop timestamp)

```{python}
#| label: latency

# Latency is only calculable if the type is TIMESTAMP and has values.
_lat_ok = False
if has_col(con, "messages", "start_timestamp") and has_col(con, "messages", "stop_timestamp"):
    _types = con.execute("DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM messages").df()
    _is_ts = "TIMESTAMP" in _types[_types["column_name"]=="start_timestamp"]["column_type"].iloc[0].upper()
    _n_lat = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(start_timestamp) FROM messages WHERE stop_timestamp IS NOT NULL").fetchone()[0]
    _lat_ok = _is_ts and _n_lat > 0

if _lat_ok:
    _lat = con.execute("""
        SELECT
            COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE stop_timestamp IS NOT NULL AND role='assistant') AS n,
            ROUND(MEDIAN(EXTRACT(epoch FROM stop_timestamp - start_timestamp))::DOUBLE, 1) AS median_s,
            ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(epoch FROM stop_timestamp - start_timestamp))::DOUBLE, 1) AS avg_s,
            ROUND(MAX(EXTRACT(epoch FROM stop_timestamp - start_timestamp))::DOUBLE, 1) AS max_s
        FROM messages
        WHERE role='assistant'
    """).df()
    show_df(_lat.assign(
        median_s=_lat["median_s"].fillna(0),
        avg_s=_lat["avg_s"].fillna(0),
        max_s=_lat["max_s"].fillna(0),
    ))
else:
    show_md("> This platform does not expose populated `start_timestamp`/`stop_timestamp`.")
```

# 3. Volumes and distributions

## 3.1 Conversations created per month (timeline + cumulative)

```{python}
#| label: timeline

_tl = con.execute("""
    SELECT strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m') AS month, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY month ORDER BY month
""").df()

if len(_tl):
    _tl["cumul"] = _tl["n"].cumsum()
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=_tl["month"], y=_tl["n"], name="Per month",
                         marker_color=SOURCE_COLOR, yaxis="y"))
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=_tl["month"], y=_tl["cumul"], name="Cumulative",
                             yaxis="y2", line=dict(color=MUTED, width=2)))
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"Conversations created per month ({len(_tl)} months covered)",
        height=380, template="plotly_white",
        xaxis=dict(type="category"),
        yaxis=dict(title="Per month"),
        yaxis2=dict(title="Cumulative", overlaying="y", side="right"),
        margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=40),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.25),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## 3.2 Activity heatmap (hour × day of week)

When you use the platform. Rows = day of week (0=Monday),
columns = hour of day (0-23).

```{python}
#| label: heatmap

_hm = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        EXTRACT(isodow FROM created_at) AS dow,  -- 1=Monday, 7=Sunday
        EXTRACT(hour FROM created_at) AS hour,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY dow, hour
""").df()

if len(_hm):
    _pivot = _hm.pivot(index="dow", columns="hour", values="n").fillna(0)
    _days = ["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"]
    _pivot = _pivot.reindex(range(1,8))
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
        z=_pivot.values, x=list(_pivot.columns), y=_days,
        colorscale=[[0, "#ffffff"], [1, SOURCE_COLOR]],
        showscale=True,
    ))
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Activity — convs created by hour × day",
        height=300, template="plotly_white",
        xaxis=dict(title="Hour", dtick=2),
        margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=60, r=20),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## 3.3 Messages by role (visible vs hidden)

```{python}
#| label: msgs-by-role

_role = con.execute("""
    SELECT role,
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT is_hidden) AS visible,
        SUM(is_hidden::INT) AS hidden,
        ROUND(AVG(word_count), 1) AS avg_words
    FROM messages GROUP BY role ORDER BY role
""").df()
show_df(_role)
```

## 3.4 Word count: user vs assistant

Word distribution per message, side by side.

```{python}
#| label: word-count-user-asst

_wc = con.execute("""
    SELECT role,
        ROUND(AVG(word_count), 0)::INT AS avg_words,
        MEDIAN(word_count)::INT AS median_words,
        MAX(word_count) AS max_words,
        SUM(word_count) AS total_words
    FROM messages
    WHERE NOT is_hidden AND word_count > 0
    GROUP BY role ORDER BY role
""").df()
show_df(_wc)
```

## 3.5 Tool events by category

```{python}
#| label: events-by-type

if table_count(con, "tool_events") > 0:
    _et_dist = con.execute("""
        SELECT event_type, COUNT(*) AS n,
               COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) AS n_convs
        FROM tool_events GROUP BY event_type ORDER BY n DESC
    """).df()
    show_df(_et_dist)

    fig = plotly_bar(
        _et_dist["event_type"].tolist(), _et_dist["n"].tolist(),
        title="ToolEvents by category",
        color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=350,
    )
    fig.show()

    # Success rate (CLIs)
    if has_col(con, "tool_events", "success"):
        _sr = con.execute("""
            SELECT
                SUM(CASE WHEN success THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS success,
                SUM(CASE WHEN success = false THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS error,
                SUM(CASE WHEN success IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unknown,
                COUNT(*) AS total
            FROM tool_events
        """).df()
        _t = int(_sr["total"].iloc[0])
        show_df(pd.DataFrame([
            {"Status": "Success", "N": fmt_pct(safe_int(_sr['success'].iloc[0]), _t)},
            {"Status": "Error",   "N": fmt_pct(safe_int(_sr['error'].iloc[0]), _t)},
            {"Status": "Unknown", "N": fmt_pct(safe_int(_sr['unknown'].iloc[0]), _t)},
        ]))

    # Duration (Codex)
    if has_col(con, "tool_events", "duration_ms"):
        _dur = con.execute("""
            SELECT
                COUNT(duration_ms) AS n,
                ROUND(AVG(duration_ms))::INT AS avg_ms,
                MEDIAN(duration_ms)::INT AS median_ms,
                MAX(duration_ms) AS max_ms
            FROM tool_events
            WHERE duration_ms IS NOT NULL
        """).df()
        if int(_dur["n"].iloc[0]) > 0:
            show_df(_dur)
else:
    show_md("> No tool_events on this platform.")
```

## 3.6 Top tools (by calls)

```{python}
#| label: top-tools

if table_count(con, "tool_events") > 0:
    _tt = con.execute("""
        SELECT tool_name, COUNT(*) AS calls
        FROM tool_events
        WHERE tool_name IS NOT NULL AND tool_name != ''
        GROUP BY tool_name ORDER BY calls DESC LIMIT 15
    """).df()

    if len(_tt):
        fig = plotly_bar(
            _tt["tool_name"].tolist()[::-1],
            _tt["calls"].tolist()[::-1],
            f"Top {len(_tt)} tools by calls",
            color=SOURCE_COLOR, orientation="h", height=450,
        )
        fig.show()
        show_df(_tt)
```

## 3.7 Top 10 models

```{python}
#| label: top-models

_top = con.execute("""
    SELECT model, COUNT(*) AS n FROM conversations
    WHERE model IS NOT NULL GROUP BY model ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 10
""").df()
if len(_top):
    fig = plotly_bar(
        _top["model"].tolist(), _top["n"].tolist(),
        title="Top 10 models (Conversation.model)",
        color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=350,
    )
    fig.show()
    show_df(_top)
else:
    show_md("> This platform does not record `model` on conversations.")
```

## 3.8 Top 10 longest conversations

```{python}
#| label: top-longest

_tl = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        substr(conversation_id, 1, 8) AS conv_id,
        title, message_count AS msgs, model, project,
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created
    FROM conversations
    ORDER BY message_count DESC LIMIT 10
""").df()
show_df(_tl)
```

## 3.9 Size distribution (msgs per conv)

```{python}
#| label: size-distribution

_sz = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN message_count <= 5 THEN '1-5'
            WHEN message_count <= 10 THEN '6-10'
            WHEN message_count <= 20 THEN '11-20'
            WHEN message_count <= 50 THEN '21-50'
            WHEN message_count <= 100 THEN '51-100'
            WHEN message_count <= 200 THEN '101-200'
            ELSE '200+'
        END AS bin,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations GROUP BY bin
""").df()
_order = ['1-5','6-10','11-20','21-50','51-100','101-200','200+']
_sz['bin'] = pd.Categorical(_sz['bin'], categories=_order, ordered=True)
_sz = _sz.sort_values('bin')

fig = plotly_bar(
    _sz["bin"].astype(str).tolist(), _sz["n"].tolist(),
    title="Conversation size distribution",
    color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=300,
)
fig.update_xaxes(title="N msgs per conv")
fig.update_yaxes(title="N convs")
fig.show()

_st = con.execute("""
    SELECT MIN(message_count) AS min, MAX(message_count) AS max,
           ROUND(AVG(message_count), 1) AS avg, MEDIAN(message_count)::INT AS median
    FROM conversations
""").df()
show_df(_st)
```

## 3.10 Conv lifetime (updated_at − created_at)

How much time passes between creating the conv and the last update. One-shot
(< 1 day) vs conversations that became a project (> 7 days).

```{python}
#| label: lifetime

_lf = con.execute("""
    WITH lt AS (
        SELECT EXTRACT(epoch FROM updated_at - created_at) / 86400 AS days
        FROM conversations
        WHERE updated_at IS NOT NULL AND created_at IS NOT NULL
    )
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN days < 0.04167 THEN '< 1h'
            WHEN days < 1 THEN '1h-1d'
            WHEN days < 7 THEN '1-7 days'
            WHEN days < 30 THEN '7-30 days'
            WHEN days < 90 THEN '30-90 days'
            ELSE '> 90 days'
        END AS bin,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM lt GROUP BY bin
""").df()
_lf_order = ['< 1h','1h-1d','1-7 days','7-30 days','30-90 days','> 90 days']
_lf['bin'] = pd.Categorical(_lf['bin'], categories=_lf_order, ordered=True)
_lf = _lf.sort_values('bin')

fig = plotly_bar(
    _lf["bin"].astype(str).tolist(), _lf["n"].tolist(),
    title="Conversation lifetime (created_at → updated_at)",
    color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=300,
)
fig.show()
```

## 3.11 Branches: forks per conv

```{python}
#| label: branches-forks

if table_count(con, "branches") > 0:
    _bd = con.execute("""
        WITH counts AS (
            SELECT conversation_id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM branches GROUP BY conversation_id
        )
        SELECT n AS branches_per_conv, COUNT(*) AS n_convs FROM counts
        GROUP BY n ORDER BY n
    """).df()
    show_df(_bd)
    fig = plotly_bar(
        _bd["branches_per_conv"].astype(str).tolist(), _bd["n_convs"].tolist(),
        title="Convs by number of branches (1 = no fork)",
        color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=300,
    )
    fig.show()
```

## 3.12 Account breakdown (multi-account)

```{python}
#| label: account-breakdown

if has_col(con, "conversations", "account") and ACCOUNT_FILTER is None:
    _n_acc = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT account) FROM conversations WHERE account IS NOT NULL").fetchone()[0]
    if _n_acc > 1:
        _ab = con.execute("""
            SELECT
                COALESCE(account, '(no account)') AS account,
                COUNT(*) AS convs,
                SUM(message_count) AS total_msgs,
                ROUND(AVG(message_count), 1) AS avg_msgs
            FROM conversations GROUP BY account ORDER BY convs DESC
        """).df()
        show_df(_ab)
    else:
        show_md(f"> Single account in this base (`account`={con.execute('SELECT account FROM conversations LIMIT 1').fetchone()[0]}).")
elif ACCOUNT_FILTER is not None:
    show_md(f"> Filtered to `account = '{ACCOUNT_FILTER}'`.")
else:
    show_md("> Platform without populated `account`.")
```

# 4. Preservation and special states

## 4.1 Active vs preserved_missing

Convs **active on the server** vs **preserved locally** (disappeared from
the server but still in the merged).

```{python}
#| label: preservation-counts

_pres = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        CASE WHEN is_preserved_missing THEN 'Preserved missing' ELSE 'Active' END AS status,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        MIN(last_seen_in_server) AS first_seen,
        MAX(last_seen_in_server) AS last_seen
    FROM conversations GROUP BY is_preserved_missing
""").df()
show_df(_pres)

_n_pres = con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations WHERE is_preserved_missing").fetchone()[0]
if _n_pres > 0:
    _pl = con.execute("""
        SELECT
            substr(conversation_id, 1, 8) AS conv_id,
            title, message_count AS msgs,
            strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created,
            strftime(last_seen_in_server, '%Y-%m-%d') AS last_seen
        FROM conversations WHERE is_preserved_missing
        ORDER BY last_seen_in_server LIMIT 50
    """).df()
    show_df(_pl)
```

## 4.2 Special states (pinned / archived / temporary)

```{python}
#| label: special-states

_st = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        SUM(is_pinned::INT) AS pinned,
        SUM(is_archived::INT) AS archived,
        SUM(is_temporary::INT) AS temporary,
        COUNT(*) AS total
    FROM conversations
""").df()
_total = int(_st["total"].iloc[0])
_p = safe_int(_st["pinned"].iloc[0])
_a = safe_int(_st["archived"].iloc[0])
_te = safe_int(_st["temporary"].iloc[0])

show_df(pd.DataFrame([
    {"State": "Pinned",    "N": fmt_pct(_p, _total)},
    {"State": "Archived",  "N": fmt_pct(_a, _total)},
    {"State": "Temporary", "N": fmt_pct(_te, _total)},
]))
```

## 4.3 Full filterable table

```{python}
#| label: full-table
#| code-summary: "All conversations (itables — search/sort/paging)"
#| column: page

from itables import show as itable_show

_full = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        substr(conversation_id, 1, 8) AS conv_id,
        title, project, model,
        message_count AS msgs,
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created,
        strftime(updated_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS updated,
        is_preserved_missing AS preserved,
        is_pinned AS pinned
    FROM conversations
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
""").df()

itable_show(
    _full,
    paging=True, searching=True, ordering=True, info=True,
    lengthMenu=[10, 25, 50, 100],
    classes="display compact",
)
```
